items.md: # UNCLE SCROOGE GOES TO DISNEYLAND (Dell)
items.value: UNCLE SCROOGE GOES TO DISNEYLAND (Dell)

items.md: ## 1 - (August) 1957 - 100 pages
items.value: 1 - (August) 1957 - 100 pages

items.md: Untitled story - 20 - Scrooge tells Grandma of his days as the skipper of a steamboat, when he and Ratchet Gearloose, Gyro’s grandfather, raced the Beagle family’s steamboat to pick up a cargo of gold bound for New Orleans. *(Jan. 10, 1957)*
items.value: Untitled story - 20 - Scrooge tells Grandma of his days as the skipper of a steamboat, when he and Ratchet Gearloose, Gyro’s grandfather, raced the Beagle family’s steamboat to pick up a cargo of gold bound for New Orleans. (Jan. 10, 1957)

items.md: These pages are three panels deep.
items.value: These pages are three panels deep.

items.md:
This story is actually a segment of the story “Uncle Scrooge Goes to Disneyland,” which takes up the entire book; the Barks segment begins on the third page, not counting the covers.
Barks’s list shows this story as nineteen pages, but this may be an error; the story does not appear to have been stretched through the enlargement and rearrangement of panels.
Reprinted: Walt Disney Comics Digest No. 28, April 1971.

items.md: # VACATION IN DISNEYLAND (Dell)
items.value: VACATION IN DISNEYLAND (Dell)

items.md: ## Four Color No. 1025 - Aug.-Oct. 1959 - 36 pages
items.value: Four Color No. 1025 - Aug.-Oct. 1959 - 36 pages

items.md: DONALD DUCK in Mastering the Matterhorn - 8 - art only - The ducks climb the Matterhorn, where they find the Beagle Boys and a diamond they’ve hidden at the top of the mountain. *(Feb. 14, 1959)*
items.value: DONALD DUCK in Mastering the Matterhorn - 8 - art only - The ducks climb the Matterhorn, where they find the Beagle Boys and a diamond they’ve hidden at the top of the mountain. (Feb. 14, 1959)

items.md:
GRANDMA DUCK AND UNCLE SCROOGE in Trail
Tycoon - 6 - art only - Scrooge brings along no provisions except money on a wagon train heading west, but he learns that Indians prefer buttons to coins. *(Feb. 14, 1959)*
Reprinted: Walt Disney Comics Digest No. 9, March 1969.
items.value:

Tycoon - 6 - art only - Scrooge brings along no provisions except money on a wagon train heading west, but he learns that Indians prefer buttons to coins. (Feb. 14, 1959)

items.md:
GYRO GEARLOOSE and GUS GOOSE on the Dream Planet - 6 - art only - Gyro and Gus find Gus’s “dream planet,” where the inhabitants are completely lazy. Gyro spoils this paradise by encouraging them to work harder to make life easier. *(Feb. 14, 1959)*
Reprinted: Walt Disney Comics Digest No. 7, January 1969.

items.md: # VACATION PARADE (Dell)
items.value: VACATION PARADE (Dell)

items.md: ## No number (No. 1) - (July) 1950 - 112 pages
items.value: No number (No. 1) - (July) 1950 - 112 pages

items.md: DONALD DUCK in Vacation Time - 33 (pp. 1-33) - Donald takes the nephews camping in a wilderness area, where they encounter a bullying camper who is careless with his cigarettes and his campfire. The camper sets fire to the woods, but saves himself by stealing Donald’s car. The ducks, trapped by the blaze, save themselves by burying themselves in trenches. The camper tells the forest rangers that the ducks started the fire. *(Jan. 5, 1950)*
items.value: DONALD DUCK in Vacation Time - 33 (pp. 1-33) - Donald takes the nephews camping in a wilderness area, where they encounter a bullying camper who is careless with his cigarettes and his campfire. The camper sets fire to the woods, but saves himself by stealing Donald’s car. The ducks, trapped by the blaze, save themselves by burying themselves in trenches. The camper tells the forest rangers that the ducks started the fire. (Jan. 5, 1950)

items.md: DONALD DUCK in Camp Counselor - 8 (pp. 102-109) - art only - Donald, humiliated in his efforts to teach the nephews and other boys woodcraft, finally fools them into believing a bearskin rug is a real bear. *(Jan. 27, 1950)*
items.value: DONALD DUCK in Camp Counselor - 8 (pp. 102-109) - art only - Donald, humiliated in his efforts to teach the nephews and other boys woodcraft, finally fools them into believing a bearskin rug is a real bear. (Jan. 27, 1950)

items.md: DONALD’S GRANDMA DUCK - 14 - (pp. 114-127) - art only - The nephews, visiting Grandma, have trouble adjusting to country ways, especially the quiet at night, but Grandma is not sympathetic. They find a letter from one of Grandma’s creditors and mistakenly think that she is about to lose her farm. *(Jan. 19, 1950)*

8 - (July) 1957 - 100 pages

items.md:
(Retitled *Picnic Party*)
GYRO GEARLOOSE in Picnic - 6 - Gyro goes picnicking to see what he can do to make open-air picnics more pleasant, but by the time he’s through inventing, the picnic is no longer a picnic. *(July 12, 1956)*
